results.name: Israel
results.key_finding: Israeli strikes in Gaza City on 2026-07-18 killed at least 9 Palestinians including 3 children, a fresh dated mass-casualty event on consecutive days that reinforces an already-absolute floor.

results.name: Sudan
results.key_finding: The El Obeid siege continues with roughly 500,000 residents plus 105,000 displaced people trapped and food prices up about 300%, but no new dated mass-casualty spike surfaced beyond the 07-17 record.

results.name: Russia
results.key_finding: No newly dated Russian-attributed civilian-harm incident surfaced this cycle; the most significant dated event on Russian soil (Kotovsk, 9 killed) is Ukrainian own-conduct under the acting-party rule and is logged against Ukraine, not Russia.

results.name: Afghanistan
results.watch_flag: floor-sustained; source-reliability restored, confidence raised low to medium
results.key_finding: Human Rights Watch documented that more than 17 million Afghans face severe food insecurity in 2026 and attributed part of the aid-access failure directly to Taliban restrictions on women working in humanitarian roles — the first verified in-window own-conduct evidence after last cycle recorded zero.

results.name: Myanmar
results.watch_flag: floor-sustained; evidence held at LOW confidence, institutionally uncorroborated
results.key_finding: The only new Myanmar evidence this cycle comes from a self-published Substack newsletter that could not be corroborated institutionally within the window, so it is logged at low confidence and is not used to move any score.

results.name: South Sudan
results.key_finding: The Jonglei displacement crisis continues on its existing severe trajectory, but no fresh July-dated escalation was identified within the window — the operative figures remain March-April 2026 dated.

results.name: Iran
results.key_finding: The 50 killed and 500+ injured in Iran since 2026-07-06 are harm inflicted by the United States and are logged against the United States, not scored as an Iranian own-conduct debit; Iran's own record already prices the near-floor 2.5.

results.name: Democratic Republic of the Congo
results.key_finding: The Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak reached 2,124+ cases and 828-864 deaths and is the fastest-growing on record, but the harm is disease-attributed and near-floor arithmetic forecloses any composite move.

results.name: Pakistan
results.watch_flag: DOWNWARD WATCH — casualty reporting is security-source-only and unverified into a fourth week
results.key_finding: Operation Shaaban's toll continues climbing to 71-123 depending on the tally cited, but casualties are reported overwhelmingly as militants, are entirely security-source-reported and unverified by independent monitors, and no newly dated mass-civilian-harm event surfaced.

results.name: Lebanon
results.key_finding: All dated Lebanese harm this cycle is either Israeli-inflicted or non-state-actor harm to Lebanese state personnel; neither is Lebanese state own-conduct, so the published 17.2 is confirmed on attribution grounds.

results.name: China
results.watch_flag: watch pending first documented enforcement action under the Ethnic Unity Law
results.key_finding: The Law on Promoting Ethnic Unity and Progress continues to draw UN and multi-government criticism, but no newly dated PRC enforcement or crackdown action was identified in the window beyond the law taking effect on 2026-07-01.

results.name: Venezuela
results.watch_flag: watch for aid-diversion or responder-denial evidence
results.key_finding: The June 24 twin-earthquake death toll rose to 5,119 killed, a material increase, but the harm is disaster-attributed and no dated own-conduct escalation such as aid diversion or responder denial was identified.

results.name: Cuba
results.watch_flag: DOWNWARD WATCH — no new escalation this cycle
results.key_finding: Cuba's grid crisis and political-prisoner record were both reaffirmed without any new dated escalation between 2026-07-04 and 2026-07-18, so the published 32.8 is confirmed.

results.name: Ukraine
results.watch_flag: DOWNWARD WATCH — FIRST Ukrainian own-conduct civilian-harm entry in the ledger; converts on pattern
results.key_finding: Ukrainian drone strikes on 2026-07-18 killed 7 civilian night-shift warehouse workers at Kotovsk — the first documented Ukrainian own-conduct civilian-harm event in this entity's ledger — but a single incident does not support the roughly 1.6 raw dimension points a 5-point move would require.

results.name: United States
results.watch_flag: STRONG DOWNWARD WATCH — accumulating interstate own-conduct debit, assessed at -2.8 against a -5.0 threshold
results.key_finding: The US strike campaign in Iran is genuine, correctly attributed US own-conduct that was never priced into the 17.5 baseline, and it moves the score downward — but the magnitude the evidence honestly supports is about -2.8 points, below the 5-point proposal threshold.

adjudications.ukraine: First Ukrainian own-conduct civilian-harm entry in the ledger (Kotovsk, 7 civilian night-shift workers killed 2026-07-18, acknowledged by Zelensky). Attribution settled against Ukraine. Magnitude assessed at approximately -1.4 points against a -5.0 threshold. CONFIRM with downward watch. Separate un-re-derived domestic-governance scope gap routed to flag-for-review, not encoded as a proposal.
adjudications.united-states: US strike campaign in Iran is correctly attributed US own-conduct, never priced into the 2026-06-09 baseline of 17.5 (which was set on domestic immigration-enforcement grounds a month before the campaign began). Double-count guard clears. Honest subdimension scoring yields -0.9 raw dimension points, composite 17.5 to 14.7, delta -2.8 — below the 5-point threshold. CONFIRM with STRONG downward watch and four named conversion triggers.

rotation_state_corrections.reason: rotation-state disagreed with published index; corrected to published value per integrity rule
